title: AppState::broadcast fans out to all live clients before publishing to bus — partial delivery to cross-instance peers on error
description: AppState::broadcast() delivers to local clients via join_all, then publishes to the message bus. If join_all succeeds but bus.publish fails (bus publish failed warning logged), local clients have already received the message but cross-instance clients have not. The bus failure is silently logged at warn level with no retry and no error return to the caller. For the LocalBus (single process), this is a no-op. For a RedisBus deployment, a Redis outage causes partial delivery: local instances delivered, remote instances missed. The caller at the ws.rs BROADCAST handler has no way to detect this — it always sends a BroadcastResult success response. Users on remote relay instances silently miss messages.
